exemplar.title: Analyzing the Correlation and Predictive Models Between 100m and 200m Sprint Performances Using Statistical Methods and Regression Analysis

detailedExemplarData.title: Analyzing the Correlation and Predictive Models Between 100m and 200m Sprint Performances Using Statistical Methods and Regression Analysis

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical development with well-defined introduction, analysis and conclusion.
Comprehensive table of contents and numbered sections aiding navigation.
Consistent use of headings and labeled tables/figures for organization.
weaknesses: Unnecessary personal digressions disrupt the mathematical thread.
Inconsistent figure/table numbering and occasional linguistic inaccuracies.
Some sections lack conciseness, with repeated explanations and split tables.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is solid (Band 3) with clear introduction, body and conclusion. Headings and a detailed table of contents support coherence, but personal narratives and formatting inconsistencies prevent the seamless flow required for a top mark. Several linguistic and organizational lapses limit conciseness and polish, placing this work firmly at a score of 3/4.

strengths: Correct use of standard statistical notation (r, rÂ², MSE, Hâ, Hâ, p-value).
Effective deployment of multiple representations (scatter plots, regression lines, box plots).
Accompanying explanations ensure that mathematical arguments are understandable.
weaknesses: Inconsistent labels (e.g., âGraph 2.0â used twice) and occasional spelling errors (e.g., âinterguartileâ).
Missing units on some axes and inconsistent symbol usage in formulas.
Limited sophistication in representations (no residual plot, cramped axis scales).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly accurate, and multiple representations support the analysis (Band 3). Minor inconsistencies in labeling, spelling, units and symbol use prevent a higher score. While communication is clear overall, the absence of certain conventions and advanced representations restricts this criterion to 3/4.

strengths: Clear personal voice linking the investigation to the studentâs athletic experience.
Selection of a bespoke data set demonstrates genuine curiosity.
Introduction of non-linear models (quadratic and cubic) shows initiative beyond basic requirements.
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows a fairly standard correlationâregression path.
Personal narrative occasionally interrupts rather than enhances the mathematics.
No novel mathematical techniques beyond syllabus content.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration stems from personal interest and exhibits clear first-person engagement with appropriate initiative (Band 2). However, the mathematical journey remains standard and personal reflections sometimes impede focus, so it does not meet the threshold for outstanding engagement required for a 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on statistical measures in relation to training outcomes.
Acknowledgement of model limitations (e.g., unrealistic cubic predictions).
Clear linkage between mathematical findings and personal understanding of sprint performance.
weaknesses: Reflections are mostly descriptive rather than deeply critical.
Limited exploration of underlying causes or alternative modelling approaches.
Discussion of biases (age, gender, nationality) lacks depth on impact and mitigation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student reflects on results and connects them to personal insight (Band 2), with some critique of model validity. However, reflection stops short of substantial critical analysis, and suggestions for deeper examination of assumptions and biases are not realized, capping this at 2/3.

strengths: Use of HL-level statistical tools (Pearson correlation, hypothesis test, multiple regressions, MSE) is highly relevant.
Calculations (r, rÂ², t-statistic, MSE) are mostly correct and the main regression equation is clearly stated.
Awareness of model performance (e.g., unrealistic cubic prediction) shows evaluative thinking.
weaknesses: Key formulas (Pearson r, t-statistic) are omitted, limiting mathematical rigor.
No residual plot or assumption checking presented.
Minor computational slips (rounding, unit omissions, sign mis-copies) remain.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is well-chosen and generally correct, with clear application of course-level tools, placing the work above mid-band (Band 3). Omissions of essential formulas, lack of residual analysis and small accuracy errors prevent the sophisticated rigor required for the top two bands, yielding a justified score of 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a personal athletics question, compiling a bespoke data set and extending to non-linear models show the studentâs own thinking, yet the mathematical path (correlation â regression) is fairly standard and follows textbook routines.

Explanations of what each statistic represents and how models are interpreted indicate good conceptual grasp, yet the student does not delve into residual analysis, assumption checking or advanced inference, so understanding is good in parts but not throughout.

Calculations of r, rÂ², t-statistic and MSE appear correct; the cubic model evaluation highlights an unrealistic prediction, signalling awareness of accuracy.  Still, minor slips (rounding, missing units, cubic model mis-copy of coefficient signs) mean accuracy is mostly correct rather than flawless.

Most material is relevant to the correlation/modelling aim, yet repeated autobiographical reflections and duplicated explanations of basic concepts (e.g. re-explaining what the intercept means) lengthen the report unnecessarily.  These superfluous passages prevent the work from being consistently concise.

Standard statistical notation (r, rÂ², MSE, Hâ , Hâ , p-value) is used correctly and variables are defined.  Minor slips appear (e.g. âinterguartileâ, missing units in some equations) but do not impede understanding, so language is mostly appropriate and accurate.

Explanations accompany each diagram and the narrative is understandable.  Minor inconsistencies (calling two different graphs âGraph 2.0â, switching between âUnited/Unitesâ) disrupt absolute consistency but overall clarity is maintained.

Pearson correlation, hypothesis test, linear / quadratic / cubic regression and MSE are all appropriate HL-AI statistical tools.  While not highly sophisticated, they are undoubtedly at course level and relevant to the question, earning a mid-level band.

Tables, scatter plots, regression lines, box plots and algebraic models are all employed and generally labelled.  These multiple representations support the argument effectively, though sophistication is limited (no residual plot, axis scales occasionally cramped).

The student regularly pauses to interpret statistical measures and relate them to training experience, showing reflection beyond mere computation.  However, comments are descriptive rather than deeply critical, so reflection is meaningful but not substantial.

Introducing cubic and quadratic alternatives and computing MSE adds some initiative beyond the simplest model, yet the methods remain within ordinary syllabus content and are executed without novel techniques.  Creativity is evident but not outstanding.

The investigation follows the classic IA flow â introduction, data collection, analysis, evaluation and conclusion â and the reader can follow the mathematical story without difficulty.  Headings, sub-headings and transitions generally make the logical progression evident.  However, several sections (e.g. long personal digressions in 4.1 and 4.5) interrupt the mathematical thread and occasional linguistic inaccuracies weaken the overall polish, so the development is clear rather than exemplary.

There is some critique â e.g., noting that the cubic prediction of 15.7 s is unrealistic, acknowledging limitations such as gender and nationality â but analysis stops short of probing causes, validity of assumptions or alternative statistical tests in depth.

Reflections are explicitly linked to the studentâs understanding of sprint performance and training choices, demonstrating a clear (though not enhanced) connection between mathematics and personal insight.

A complete table of contents, numbered sections and labelled tables / figures show solid organisation.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all present and in the expected order.  Flow between major parts is serviceable but not âseamlessâ: some sections (especially 4.5 and 5.0) feel bolted on and the data table is split awkwardly over two pages.  Hence the work is well-organised rather than exceptional.

Frequent first-person comments link the work to the studentâs experience and the investigation clearly stems from personal curiosity.  This personal voice drives the exploration but does not significantly change or enhance the mathematics itself.

annotations.comment: The correlation section defines r from rÂ² via square root but omits the Pearson formula. Include $$r=\frac{\sum(x-\bar x)(y-\bar y)}{\sqrt{\sum(x-\bar x)^2\sum(y-\bar y)^2}}$$.
Graph is labelled as âGraph 1.0â here but referred to as âGraph 2.0â elsewhere. Maintain consistent figure numbering.
Hypothesis statements are clearly structured with $$H_0:\,\rho=0$$ and $$H_1:\,\rho\neq0$$, demonstrating precise mathematical communication.
The t-statistic calculation is presented but lacks the formula $$t=\frac{r\sqrt{n-2}}{\sqrt{1-r^2}}$$. Include it to enhance rigor.
The title and exploration heading are prominently displayed, providing a clear focus for the investigation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly lists all sections, aiding navigation through the exploration.
The opening paragraph includes personal narrative but contains grammar errors (e.g., âdo athlete that runâ). Consider revising for clarity and conciseness.
The mathematics rationale mentions residual analysis but does not present a residual plot. Include the plot and discuss assumptions to strengthen the analysis.
The data collection section includes rich personal reflection, but it disrupts the mathematical thread. Consider moving narrative insights to a reflection section.
The data table spans both columns and is split over two pages, making it hard to follow. Reformat to a single coherent table.
Having two identical column headings (â100 Meters Timeâ and â100 Meter Timeâ) causes confusion. Ensure unique, consistent labels.
The scatter plot effectively visualizes the positive trend between 100 m and 200 m times and sets up the analysis well.
The write-up uses âinterguartileâ instead of âinterquartile.â Correct spelling to maintain precision.
Axes on both box plots lack unit labels. Label axes with units (seconds) to comply with mathematical communication standards.
Your interpretation of skew direction should refer to quartile positions rather than subjective language. Clarify how skew is determined.
Consider showing the whisker and outlier boundary calculations numerically to support the box-plot conclusions.
The t-test results table lacks a descriptive title and clear column headings. Add a caption like âTable 3.0: Linear Regression t-Test Results.â
The regression equation $$y=1.3557x+6.6901$$ is clearly stated, illustrating accurate use of course-level mathematics.
Residual analysis was mentioned but no residual plot is provided. Add the plot and interpret it to assess model assumptions.
Your personal reflection on model insights is engaging and shows clear personal engagement driving the exploration forward.
Introduction of quadratic and cubic models demonstrates initiative beyond standard linear approaches.
The interpretation of the quadratic coefficient could be clearer: explain how negative curvature affects predictions across the data range.
The cubic modelâs prediction of 15.74 s for 9.83 s 100 m is clearly unrealistic. You note this but do not discuss why the model overfits; expand your critique.
The notation in the MSE formula uses A rather than MSE, creating confusion. Use consistent symbols throughout.
MSE values are close; consider applying statistical tests (e.g., ANOVA) to confirm that the cubic model is significantly better.
The conclusion contains a typo (â299m sprint timesâ). Correct to â200 mâ to ensure precision.
Limitations list age, gender and nationality but lacks discussion on how these impact model validity. Elaborate on potential biases and future approaches.
Bibliographic entries lack a consistent citation style. Adopt a formal format (e.g., APA) to enhance presentation quality.
